2604.21365.title: mcdok at SemEval-2026 Task 13: Finetuning LLMs for Detection of Machine-Generated Code
2604.21365.paper_summary: Multi-domain detection of the machine-generated code snippets in various programming languages is a challenging task. SemEval-2026 Task~13 copes with this challenge in various angles, as a binary detection problem as well as attribution of the source. Specifically, its subtasks also cover generator LLM family detection, as well as a hybrid code co-generated by humans and machines, or adversarially modified codes hiding its origin. Our submitted systems adjusted the existing mdok approach (focused on machine-generated text detection) to these specific kinds of problems by exploring various base models, more suitable for code understanding. The results indicate that the submitted systems are competitive in all three subtasks. However, the margins from the top-performing systems are significant, and thus further improvements are possible.
2604.21365.paper_summary_zh: 多領域檢測各種程式語言中的機器生成代碼片段是一項具有挑戰性的任務。SemEval-2026任務~13從各個角度應對這一挑戰，作為二元檢測問題以及來源歸屬的問題。具體而言，其子任務還涵蓋生成器LLM家族的檢測，以及人類和機器共同生成的混合代碼，或對其來源進行對抗性修改的代碼。我們提交的系統調整了現有的mdok方法（專注於機器生成文本檢測），以應對這些特定類型的問題，通過探索更適合代碼理解的各種基礎模型。結果表明，提交的系統在所有三個子任務中具有競爭力。然而，與表現最佳的系統之間的差距是顯著的，因此進一步的改進是可能的。
2604.21365.author: Adam Skurla et.al.
2604.21365.authors: Adam Skurla, Dominik Macko, Jakub Simko
